api: Appfigures REST API v2 (https://api.appfigures.com/v2, reference at https://docs.appfigures.com/api/reference/v2/)
scope: Pass B full-surface expansion, re-researched against the live reference (https://docs.appfigures.com/api/reference/v2/*, fetched 2026-07-04). Every practical GET list/detail/aggregate resource with a fixture-verifiable response shape is now a stream (18 total) and every dialect-expressible mutation is a writes.json action (4 total: review response, event create/update/delete). Path-templated report resources requiring an already-known product id and/or date-range path segments (ranks, featured, ASO, per-product SDK detection), async job-poll bulk protocols, and reports whose response shape varies structurally by an optional group_by query parameter beyond the documented ungrouped-totals shape (payments, the reports/usage variant distinct from the account-level /usage) are excluded — see individual entries for exact rationale.

excluded.reason: an aggregate breakdown (stars/versions/countries/products histograms) of the same reviews the reviews stream already reads to exhaustion; a derived rollup, not additional source data

a developer's own review response is the direct counterpart of the reply_to_review write action's request body; not modeled as a separate read stream since there is no id-enumeration source for review_id independent of the reviews stream already covered, and reading back a just-written response has no distinct analytics/reverse-ETL value

generic product search across the full Appfigures product catalog (public + tracked); products/mine (already covered) is the tracked-products-only view this connector's account actually owns and reverse-ETL/analytics use cases target

per-user product-matching lookup used for onboarding/account-linking UI flows, not an analytics or configuration resource; requires an already-known user email as a path segment with no id-enumeration source independent of the users stream

the real endpoint is GET /products/{product_id}/sdks (per-product SDK detection history) — requires an already-known product_id path segment; a fan_out over the products stream's ids would work but was not prioritized for this wave

no fixture-verifiable ungrouped-totals response shape could be confirmed against the live reference during this research pass (unlike revenue/subscriptions/ads/estimates, whose flat-object totals shape is explicitly documented); shipping a guessed schema risks a SCHEMA_AMBIGUOUS-class parity error, so this is deferred rather than guessed
same fixture-verifiability gap as reports/adspend — no confirmed ungrouped response shape found in this research pass

a usage-analytics report (distinct from the account-level GET /usage credit-usage resource, which is itself covered via the account_info stream's nested usage object); no confirmed response shape found in this research pass

requires already-known product ids AND an explicit date range as PATH segments (not query params); the dialect's stream.path templating could reference config values for these but doing so would hardcode a single fixed product/date-range per connector configuration rather than reading the full account's rank history generically — not prioritized for this wave
same path-templated-parameters rationale as the basic ranks report; additionally requires a specific category/subcategory the caller must already know from the data/categories stream

keyword-tracking aggregate metrics requiring a specific product id and country as query parameters with no natural per-record list shape (it is a single summary object per product/country combination, not a general collection) — an ASO/keyword-tracking add-on feature not prioritized for this wave
keyword rank-trend detail rows scoped to a specific product id; same ASO add-on feature not prioritized for this wave as aso/stats

requires an explicit date range as path segments and returns a nested per-product/per-path breakdown with no fixed record shape (the paths array's contents vary); a featured-placement add-on feature not prioritized for this wave
requires an already-known product id AND explicit date-range path segments; same featured-placement add-on rationale as featured/summary
featured-placement add-on feature not prioritized for this wave; same rationale as the other featured endpoints
single-external-account detail GET; every field is already present on each record of the external_accounts list stream
single-user detail GET; every field is already present on each record of the users list stream
an Apple-only-filtered subset of the same country reference data data_countries already reads in full

the account_info stream's nested usage.{daily_used,daily_limit} fields (sourced from the root endpoint's own response body) already surface the same daily API credit usage; no distinct documented response shape for a standalone /usage call was found beyond what the root endpoint already returns
